title: Rosa × klasterskii
taxonomy: Rosa × klasterskii is a hybrid rose species. It is a cross between Rosa gallica and Rosa moschata. Rosa × klasterskii is a fragrant, double-flowered rose with a pink color. The classification of Rosa × klasterskii as a rose species is recognized by the Royal Horticultural Society. Rosa × klasterskii is an old garden rose that originated in the 18th century.

intro: Rosa × klasterskii, also known as the Klastersky rose, is a hybrid rose species that is widely cultivated for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The Klastersky rose has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its numerous health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Klastersky rose include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of nausea, diarrhea, and fever.
